summary: NEW FEATURE VERIFIED: Email invites for newly added participants. Backend fix confirmed - POST /api/meetings/{meeting_id}/participants now sends email invites. All 11 backend tests passed. Frontend E2E test passed - participant count increased from 4 to 5 and email sent successfully.
seed_data_creation: Test users with 'TEST_' and 'e2e_test_user_' prefix created during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Email invite feature for newly added participants is working correctly. The fix was in POST /api/meetings/{meeting_id}/participants endpoint (lines 800-863 in server.py). Email is sent via send_meeting_invite() function from utils/email.py. Backend logs confirm emails are sent with message: 'Sent meeting invite to newly added participant: <email>'

new_feature_email_invites.issue_reported: Newly added participants to existing meetings not receiving email invites
email_logs_sample: 2026-03-03 22:15:19 - Sent meeting invite to newly added participant: test_e2e_7681@hospital.com

email_contents_verified.meeting_location: Present in email template (or 'To be announced' fallback)

bug_rca.root_cause: The POST /api/meetings/{meeting_id}/participants endpoint was not calling send_meeting_invite() for newly added participants. It only added the participant record to the database.
bug_rca.fix_applied: Added email notification logic (lines 834-857) that fetches participant user details, organizer details, and meeting data, then calls send_meeting_invite() to send the invitation email.
reproduction_steps: 2. Create a meeting or go to existing future meeting
3. Click 'Add' button to add a new participant
5. Prior to fix: No email sent. After fix: Email invitation sent
bug_rca.verification: Backend logs show 'Sent meeting invite to newly added participant: <email>'
